sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: West Ham United are struggling, currently in 18th place with 13 points and winless in their last 6 matches, conceding goals consistently (no clean sheet in 14 games). Fulham, sitting 13th with 23 points, have shown mixed results but managed a win in their last match (1-0 vs Nottingham Forest) and scored in high-scoring affairs recently. Their attacking output (24 goals this season) suggests they can exploit West Ham's defensive frailties.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: Recent head-to-head encounters between West Ham and Fulham have been competitive, with the last two matches ending 3-2 (West Ham win) and 1-1. Both games saw goals from both sides, indicating a trend of high-scoring, open play when these teams meet.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Injuries
sections.text: West Ham's Jarrod Bowen (5 goals) and Lucas Paquetá remain pivotal, but missing defender Aaron Wan-Bissaka could hurt their backline. Fulham's Raúl Jiménez (3 goals) and Emile Smith Rowe are threats, though the absence of Harry Wilson (5 goals, 3 assists) and Alex Iwobi might limit their attacking options.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: West Ham’s low possession (41.9%, 19th in the league) and high clearance stats (31.35 per game, 2nd) suggest a defensive, counter-attacking approach, which struggles against Fulham’s ability to create big chances. Fulham's tendency to score in the second half (majority of goals after the 45th minute) could test West Ham's endurance.

sections.subtitle: Statistical Trends
sections.text: Both teams often see matches with goals on both ends (West Ham 7/9, Fulham 5/6 in recent games), and over 2.5 goals is common for Fulham (5/6 matches). Card counts are typically low (under 4.5 in last 5 for both), and corners are moderate (under 10.5 in recent head-to-heads).

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Given Fulham's better form, higher league standing, and West Ham's defensive issues, the away side holds a slight edge, aligning with Sofascore’s 49% probability for an away win. Expect a close, goal-heavy match, likely ending in a narrow Fulham victory due to their attacking consistency.

halftimeSections.subtitle: First half overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half at the London Stadium between West Ham United and Fulham ended goalless with a scoreline of 0-0. Both teams had their moments, but neither could find the breakthrough despite some promising attacking play. Fulham appeared more threatening in terms of shot volume, managing more efforts on target compared to West Ham's solitary attempt. However, West Ham showed resilience in defense and had decent entries into dangerous areas, hinting at potential for a more assertive second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance and statistics
halftimeSections.text: Fulham controlled possession slightly more than West Ham and showed greater intent in the final third with a higher number of entries. Their expected goals were also notably higher, suggesting they created better quality chances. West Ham, on the other hand, managed a decent number of penalty area entries and won a slight majority in overall duels, indicating a competitive edge in physicality. Defensively, both teams were solid, with Fulham dominating aerial duels and West Ham holding firm against sustained pressure.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw fluctuating attacking spells from both sides. West Ham started with early intent, pushing forward in the opening minutes before Fulham took over with a prolonged period of dominance, creating consistent pressure for a significant stretch. Midway through the half, West Ham regained some momentum with a series of attacking waves, showing intent to penetrate Fulham's defense. Towards the latter stages, Fulham again asserted control with several threatening spells, though West Ham managed to counter with occasional bursts. The momentum shifted frequently, setting the stage for an open second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players to watch
halftimeSections.text: For West Ham, Jarrod Bowen stands out as a potential game-changer in the second half, having already registered a shot on target and showing involvement in attacking plays. Crysencio Summerville also poses a threat with notable contributions in key areas. On Fulham's side, Harry Wilson was dangerous with a shot on target and high expected goals, while Raúl Jiménez remains a focal point in attack with his ability to create opportunities. Both teams have players capable of unlocking the deadlock if given space.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half outlook
halftimeSections.text: The second half promises to be intriguing as both teams have shown they can create chances, albeit without the final product in the first 45 minutes. West Ham may look to capitalize on their physicality and home advantage at the London Stadium to push for a goal, potentially focusing on set-pieces with their corner count advantage. Fulham, with their superior expected goals and final third presence, will likely continue to probe for openings, especially through their key attackers. Tactical adjustments could play a big role, with both managers possibly introducing fresh legs to shift the balance.
